title: Sequence Tasks
concept: Sequence modeling enables many-to-one (sentiment), one-to-many (captioning), and many-to-many (translation) architectures. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: The Vanilla RNN.'

exercises.prompt: What determines whether a sequence task is classified as many-to-one, one-to-many, or many-to-many?

options.text: The order and structure of the input and output data
The speed at which the model trains
The programming language used to write the AI

exercises.explain: Sequence tasks handle input data and output data that flow in sequences (like words in a sentence or frames in a video).

cards.title: Connecting the Dots
cards.body: AI doesn't just look at single pictures or numbers. It can read entire books, write sentences word-by-word, and translate languages. But how does it handle data when the inputs and outputs have different lengths? Let's explore how sequence tasks shape our AI's mind!

cards.title: What is Sequence Modeling?
cards.body: Sequence modeling is a way to handle data where order matters, like sentences or music. Depending on the task, models can map a single input to multiple outputs (one-to-many), multiple inputs to a single output (many-to-one), or multiple inputs to multiple outputs (many-to-many).

cards.title: The Post Office of Data
cards.body:
Imagine a sequence model as a post office. 

Sometimes you send one letter and get one back. 
Sometimes you send a single photo, and the post office delivers it to multiple friends (one-to-many). 
Sometimes a whole group of friends sends letters, and you summarize them in one response (many-to-many). 

The model adapts its mailbox structure to fit how inputs and outputs flow!

cards.title: Real-World Sequence Tasks
cards.body:
Here are the classic sequence tasks in action:

• One-to-Many: You feed in one photo of a dog, and the model outputs a sequence of words: "A brown dog running."
• Many-to-One: You feed in a sequence of movie review words, and the model outputs a single "5-star" rating.
• Many-to-Many: You feed in a sentence in English, and the model outputs the translated sentence in Spanish.

cards.title: Myth: One Input, One Output
cards.body: People often think AI models can only connect one input to one output. In reality, modern sequence models are highly flexible! They can take a single input and generate a long sequence, or take a long sequence and compress it into a single output label.

cards.title: Sequence Layouts Compared
cards.body: Depending on what you want to build, you will choose a different sequence layout to match your input and output needs.

left.text: Takes a sequence of words and outputs a single sentiment score.

right.text: Takes a sequence of words and outputs a translated sequence of words.

exercises.prompt: Which sequence task structure takes a single input (like an image) and produces a sequence of outputs (like a caption sentence)?
exercises.explain: Image captioning takes a single image (one) and generates a sequence of words describing it (many).

exercises.prompt: True or False: Translating a sentence from English to French is a many-to-many sequence task.
exercises.explain: Language translation is a many-to-many task because it takes a sequence of words in the source language and outputs a sequence of words in the target language.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of this classification task.
exercises.sentence: When a model reads a sequence of words to predict whether a tweet is happy or sad, it is using a [blank] architecture.
exercises.explain: Since we are feeding in a sequence of words (many) and getting a single classification score (one), this is a classic many-to-one task.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a One-to-Many sequence task?

options.text: A model that takes a single melody note and generates a whole song.
A model that translates a Spanish sentence into English.
A model that reads a book review and outputs a thumbs-up.

exercises.explain: Option B is incorrect because translating a full sentence into another full sentence requires reading a sequence of words and writing a sequence of words (many-to-many). Option C is incorrect because rating a movie from a review is many-to-one. Option A correctly represents one-to-many.

exercises.prompt: A customer review consists of a sequence of 50 words, and the model must output a single classification: 'Positive' or 'Negative'. What type of sequence task is this?
exercises.explain: Amazing job! You've mastered how sequence tasks handle inputs and outputs in different shapes—whether it's many-to-one, one-to-many, or many-to-many. Next up: The Vanilla RNN.
